spec: shows just a number starting at 0 and buttons labeled "1", "2", "3"; clicking a digit appends it (the number becomes ten times itself plus the digit)
shows "step 1" and buttons labeled "next" and "back" that move between "step 1", "step 2", and "step 3" without going past either end
a tiny adding machine: buttons "2" and "3" set the current entry to that digit, "plus" stores the entry and clears it, "=" shows just the sum of the stored value and the entry
has a button labeled "+1" adding 1 to a hidden count starting 0; shows just the count only when it is even, otherwise shows the text "odd"
has buttons "a+" and "b+" each adding 1 to its own hidden count, and shows just the FIRST count minus the SECOND (which can be negative)
shows just a number starting at 0, a button "+5" that adds 5, and a button "undo" that restores the value from before the most recent "+5"
shows just a number starting at 0 and a button "step": stepping adds 2 while going up, but after reaching 6 it turns around and subtracts 2 until 0, and so on
has a text input and a button "login"; the text "Welcome back" appears only after clicking "login" while the input is not empty
shows just a total starting at 0, a button "add 3" that adds 3, and separately the text "even" or "odd" for the total's parity
has a button "mode" toggling between "view" (the start) and "edit"; shows the current mode name, and a text input plus "typing: <text>" are present only in edit mode
has buttons "arm" and "launch"; "LIFTOFF" appears only if "launch" is clicked while armed (clicking "launch" unarmed does nothing)
has buttons "add 4" and "add 8" that add to a hidden running total while counting how many were added; shows just the integer average (0 before any adds)
shows just a number starting at 1 and a button "next" that cycles it 1, 2, 3 and back to 1
shows just a number starting at 1 and a button "double" that doubles it, but any result over 100 becomes 1 again
has two text inputs (first name then last name) and shows them as "<last>, <first>"
shows "coins: <n>" starting 0, a button "insert coin" adding 1, and a button "buy" that only when there are at least 2 coins spends 2 and shows the text "dispensed"
